message:
fix(release): use macos-15-intel runner for x86_64 Darwin builds

The macOS Intel binary (pi-darwin-amd64) was missing from releases because
the x86_64-apple-darwin target was assigned to the macos-15 runner (Apple
Silicon). This required fragile cross-compilation. Switch to the native
macos-15-intel runner so the build produces a real x86_64 binary, and drop
the unused `cross: true` matrix flag.

Fixes #30

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -112,10 +112,9 @@ jobs:
           - os: ubuntu-24.04-arm
             target: aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu
             asset: pi-linux-arm64
-          - os: macos-15
+          - os: macos-15-intel
             target: x86_64-apple-darwin
             asset: pi-darwin-amd64
-            cross: true
           - os: macos-15
             target: aarch64-apple-darwin
             asset: pi-darwin-arm64
